title: Long-Lost Morecambe and Wise Episode Rediscovered and Set to Air
summary: A missing episode of the iconic British comedy duo Morecambe and Wise, originally broadcast on September 16, 1968, has been recovered from a private collection. The episode is part of their return to the BBC after a stint with ATV. The copy was preserved by a deceased industry professional. The BBC plans to broadcast it in the coming months. Professor Justin Smith, chair of trustees at Film is Fabulous! (FIF), the charitable trust that tracked it down, describes it as fast-paced and witty, featuring trademark sketches like a balloon dancer and a nudist camp scene that reflect the 'permissive society' of the era. Smith notes that the duo were in their prime. The recording is in black and white, but FIF believes it can be restored to original quality. This discovery adds to the legacy of Morecambe and Wise, who remain beloved in British comedy.

background_read: Morecambe and Wise were a British comedy duo active from the 1940s to the 1980s. Their BBC show was hugely popular, with Christmas specials drawing millions of viewers. Many early episodes were lost because the BBC reused tapes. Charities like Film is Fabulous! work to find and restore lost TV shows.
Article_Structure: Main Points: A lost 1968 Morecambe and Wise episode was found in a private collection and will be broadcast. Purpose: To inform readers about the rediscovery and upcoming broadcast. Evidence Evaluation: The article cites Professor Justin Smith, a credible academic, and the organization FIF. Author Credibility: The BBC is a reputable news source. Methodology: The episode was tracked down by FIF, a trust of collectors and enthusiasts.

perspectives.description: Value the episode as a cultural artifact from a transformative era.
Excited to see classic comedy that defined an era.
